message: fix: Resolve all linter, typecheck, and unused variable issues in web package

files.patch:
@@ -27,7 +27,7 @@
     "lint": "bunx eslint packages --max-warnings=0 --no-warn-ignored",
     "typecheck": "tsc --noEmit",
     "check": "bun run lint && bun run typecheck",
-    "test": "bun test",
+    "test": "bun test test.ts",
     "test:integration": "PHAGE_INTEGRATION=1 bun test --filter integration"
   },
   "devDependencies": {

             { key: 'N / C', desc: 'Toggle DNA / AA view' },

             { key: 'T', desc: `Theme: ${theme.name}` },
             { key: 'D', desc: `Diff mode ${diffEnabled ? '●' : '○'}` },
           ],

@@ -0,0 +1,86 @@
+{
+  "id": "overlays",
+  "title": "Analysis Deep‑Dive",
+  "description": "Learn how to open and interpret Phage Explorer’s analysis overlays.",
+  "estimatedMinutes": 3,
+  "steps": [
+    {
+      "id": "open-palette",
+      "title": "Open the command palette",
+      "content": "Analyses are opened from the command palette. Press : (colon) any time to bring it up, then type an analysis name.",
+      "target": ".footer-hints",
+      "position": "top"
+    },
+    {
+      "id": "search-analyses",
+      "title": "Search for analyses",
+      "content": "Try typing “GC skew”, “complexity”, or “promoter”. The palette groups commands by category and respects your experience level.",
+      "target": ".app-body",
+      "position": "center"
+    },
+    {
+      "id": "gc-skew",
+      "title": "GC skew overlay",
+      "content": "GC skew highlights replication/packaging asymmetries. Peaks and troughs often mark origin‑like regions or structural modules.",
+      "target": ".app-body",
+      "position": "center"
+    },
+    {
+      "id": "complexity",
+      "title": "Sequence complexity",
+      "content": "Complexity (Shannon entropy) drops in low‑diversity regions such as repeats or highly constrained genes.",
+      "target": ".app-body",
+      "position": "center"
+    },
+    {
+      "id": "promoters",
+      "title": "Promoter / RBS motifs",
+      "content": "Promoter marks indicate likely transcription start regions. Clusters can suggest operons or early/late transcription blocks.",
+      "target": ".gene-map-container",
+      "position": "bottom"
+    },
+    {
+      "id": "repeats",
+      "title": "Repeats / palindromes",
+      "content": "Repeat overlays flag palindromic or duplicated motifs. These can be recombination sites or regulatory hairpins.",
+      "target": ".app-body",
+      "position": "center"
+    },
+    {
+      "id": "dotplot",
+      "title": "Dot plot & self‑similarity",
+      "content": "Dot plots show internal repeats and block duplications. Diagonal stripes off the main diagonal indicate duplicated segments.",
+      "target": ".app-body",
+      "position": "center"
+    },
+    {
+      "id": "cgr-hilbert",
+      "title": "CGR & Hilbert maps",
+      "content": "CGR (Chaos Game Representation) and Hilbert curves visualize k‑mer signatures. Similar phages cluster in pattern space.",
+      "target": ".app-body",
+      "position": "center"
+    },
+    {
+      "id": "hgt",
+      "title": "HGT tracer",
+      "content": "Horizontal gene transfer hotspots are flagged by atypical k‑mer and codon signatures. Use this to spot modular swaps.",
+      "target": ".app-body",
+      "position": "center"
+    },
+    {
+      "id": "tropism",
+      "title": "Tropism & receptors",
+      "content": "Tropism overlays summarize predicted receptor targets and tail‑fiber signals. High‑confidence hits show likely host range.",
+      "target": ".app-body",
+      "position": "center"
+    },
+    {
+      "id": "next-steps",
+      "title": "Learn the theory",
+      "content": "If any concept feels unfamiliar, open Beginner Mode’s Learn menu for foundational modules and glossary links.",
+      "target": "button[aria-haspopup=\"true\"]",
+      "position": "bottom"
+    }
+  ]
+}
+

@@ -0,0 +1,58 @@
+{
+  "id": "gene-explorer",
+  "title": "Gene Explorer Tour",
+  "description": "Focus on gene‑level features: ORFs, start/stop codons, and protein‑coding regions.",
+  "estimatedMinutes": 2,
+  "steps": [
+    {
+      "id": "gene-map",
+      "title": "Genes on the map",
+      "content": "The gene map shows predicted genes on forward (top) and reverse (bottom) strands.",
+      "target": ".gene-map-container",
+      "position": "bottom"
+    },
+    {
+      "id": "jump-to-gene",
+      "title": "Jump to a gene",
+      "content": "Click a gene region in the map to jump the sequence viewer to that position.",
+      "target": ".gene-map-container",
+      "position": "bottom"
+    },
+    {
+      "id": "sequence-region",
+      "title": "Reading an ORF",
+      "content": "An ORF (open reading frame) is a stretch of DNA that can be translated into a protein. Look for long regions without stop codons in a frame.",
+      "target": "canvas[aria-label=\"Genome sequence canvas\"]",
+      "position": "top"
+    },
+    {
+      "id": "start-stop",
+      "title": "Start and stop codons",
+      "content": "Start codons (usually ATG) mark translation start; stop codons (TAA/TAG/TGA) terminate translation. In AA view, stops appear as *.",
+      "target": ".view-mode-toggle",
+      "position": "bottom"
+    },
+    {
+      "id": "reading-frames",
+      "title": "Reading frames matter",
+      "content": "Switch to Amino Acids and press f to cycle frames. A real gene will show a coherent protein in one frame and nonsense in others.",
+      "target": ".view-mode-toggle",
+      "position": "bottom"
+    },
+    {
+      "id": "protein-properties",
+      "title": "Protein properties",
+      "content": "Amino acids are colored by chemistry (hydrophobic, polar, acidic, basic). Patterns can hint at membrane proteins or enzymes.",
+      "target": "canvas[aria-label=\"Genome sequence canvas\"]",
+      "position": "top"
+    },
+    {
+      "id": "codon-usage",
+      "title": "Codon usage",
+      "content": "Use the command palette (:) to open Codon Usage or Bias overlays. Skewed usage can indicate host adaptation or horizontal transfer.",
+      "target": ".footer-hints",
+      "position": "top"
+    }
+  ]
+}
+

@@ -0,0 +1,79 @@
+{
+  "id": "welcome",
+  "title": "Welcome to Phage Explorer",
+  "description": "A quick tour of the web UI: pick a phage, read its genome, and open analyses.",
+  "estimatedMinutes": 2,
+  "steps": [
+    {
+      "id": "header",
+      "title": "Header and status",
+      "content": "This top bar shows where you are, your keyboard mode, and quick actions like Theme, Contrast, and Beginner Mode.",
+      "target": ".app-header",
+      "position": "bottom"
+    },
+    {
+      "id": "phage-list",
+      "title": "Phage list",
+      "content": "Browse included phages here. Each entry shows host, genome length, lifecycle, and GC%. Click any phage to load it.",
+      "target": ".column--list .list",
+      "position": "right"
+    },
+    {
+      "id": "select-phage",
+      "title": "Selecting a phage",
+      "content": "The highlighted row is your current selection. Use j/k to move fast, or click another phage.",
+      "target": ".list-item.active, .list-item",
+      "position": "right"
+    },
+    {
+      "id": "details-pane",
+      "title": "Details pane",
+      "content": "Key metadata lives here: genome length, GC content, gene count, lifecycle, and accession.",
+      "target": ".column--detail .detail-card",
+      "position": "left"
+    },
+    {
+      "id": "gene-map",
+      "title": "Gene map",
+      "content": "This map shows gene locations and strands. Click anywhere to jump the sequence viewer to that region.",
+      "target": ".gene-map-container",
+      "position": "bottom"
+    },
+    {
+      "id": "sequence-canvas",
+      "title": "Sequence grid",
+      "content": "Scroll to move through the genome. Letters are color‑coded by nucleotide or amino‑acid chemistry.",
+      "target": "canvas[aria-label=\"Genome sequence canvas\"]",
+      "position": "top"
+    },
+    {
+      "id": "view-modes",
+      "title": "DNA vs amino acids",
+      "content": "Toggle between DNA, Amino Acids, or Dual view. In AA mode, press f to cycle reading frames.",
+      "target": ".view-mode-toggle",
+      "position": "bottom"
+    },
+    {
+      "id": "navigation",
+      "title": "Navigation shortcuts",
+      "content": "Footer hints show the core hotkeys. Try / for search, ? for help, and Home/End to jump genome ends.",
+      "target": ".footer-hints",
+      "position": "top"
+    },
+    {
+      "id": "analysis",
+      "title": "Open analyses",
+      "content": "Press : to open the command palette, then search for analyses like GC skew, complexity, promoters, dot plots, and more.",
+      "target": ".key-hint",
+      "position": "top"
+    },
+    {
+      "id": "learn-next",
+      "title": "Go deeper",
+      "content": "With Beginner Mode on, the Learn menu opens foundational modules and the glossary whenever you need context.",
+      "target": "button[aria-haspopup=\"true\"]",
+      "position": "bottom"
+    }
+  ]
+}
+
